response:
# Verdict: REJECT ## Overall Assessment This popover component has **critical accessibility failures** in its core interaction model. It implements a dialog pattern but lacks all essential ARIA attributes for assistive technology communication, no focus management design, and missing state synchronization with screen readers. The component will fail real users of assistive technology despite passing automated checks (which would likely flag the obvious issues anyway). ## Pre-commitment Predictions | Prediction | Status | Notes | |------------|--------|-------| | Missing role="dialog" on popover content | ✅ Confirmed | Critical pattern gap | | No focus trap implementation | ✅ Confirmed | WCAG 2.1.2 violation | | Focus not restored to trigger on close | ✅ Confirmed | Design is broken, not accidental | | No aria-labelledby for dialog labeling | ✅ Confirmed | State communication missing | | Escape key handling absent | ✅ Confirmed | Keyboard trap risk | ## Critical Findings (blocks access) ### 1. Missing Dialog Role and Modal Attribute ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:20-23
``` **Evidence**: Line 20 - Popover content has no `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal` attribute **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) cannot identify this as a dialog region **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog pattern requires `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` **Fix Required**: Add `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, and ensure focus is trapped within the dialog ### 2. No Focus Management Design ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:7-15 const openPopover = () => { setIsOpen(true); // BUG: Focus not moved to popover or trap within it }; const closePopover = () => { setIsOpen(false); // BUG: Focus not restored to trigger button }; ``` **Evidence**: Lines 7-15 - Both handlers only toggle state without focus management **User Group Impacted**: Keyboard-only users get trapped; screen reader users lose context when content appears/disappears **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard), WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order); WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog pattern requires focus trap + restoration to trigger on close/escape **Fix Required**: Implement `use-focus-trap` or equivalent, add Escape key handler that closes and restores focus ### 3. No Accessible Name for Popover ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:20-25
{content}
``` **Evidence**: Line 21 - No `aria-labelledby` or accessible name for the dialog **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users cannot announce what this popover is about when it opens/closes **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog pattern requires labeling via `aria-labelledby` pointing to title element or button text **Fix Required**: Add `

` or `` inside popover and reference with `aria-labelledby={titleId}` ### 4. Escape Key Handler Missing ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:7-15 const closePopover = () => { setIsOpen(false); }; ``` **Evidence**: Line 8 - Close handler only toggles state, no keyboard event listener for Escape **User Group Impacted**: Keyboard-only users cannot dismiss the popover without clicking; creates trap when focus is inside dialog **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap; WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog pattern requires Escape to close and restore focus **Fix Required**: Add `onKeyDown` handler that closes on Escape key, restores focus to trigger ## Major Findings (significantly degrades experience) ### 5. Focus Not Moved When Popover Opens ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:7-10 const openPopover = () => { setIsOpen(true); }; ``` **Evidence**: Line 8 - No focus management when popover opens **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users don't know content appeared; keyboard user's focus is stuck on trigger button while new interactive elements are now in DOM and accessible **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 3.2.1 On Focus (focus doesn't cause unexpected context change); WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog pattern requires moving focus to dialog when it opens **Fix Required**: After `setIsOpen(true)`, use ref or querySelector to move focus into the popover content ### 6. No aria-describedby for Error/Status Messages ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:20-25
{content}
``` **Evidence**: Line 21 - Popover content area has no `aria-describedby` for any status/error messages **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users cannot associate validation errors or informational text with the dialog context **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages; WAI-ARIA pattern requires error/status association via `aria-describedby` **Fix Required**: If content includes status/error messages, add `id` to message element and reference in parent's `aria-describedby` ### 7. No aria-label on Close Button Context ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:23-24 ``` **Evidence**: Line 23 - "Close" button has no context about what it closes **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users don't know this is a dialog close action; could be confused with other UI elements on page **WCAG/APG Citation**: WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA pattern recommends contextual labeling for secondary actions in dialogs **Fix Required**: Consider wrapping button in `` or adding `aria-describedby={dialogTitleId}` to provide context ## Minor Findings (friction but workaround exists) ### 8. No Skip Link Context ```jsx // src/components/BuggyPopover.tsx:16-20
